title: pypodman: boolean options require values
body:
```
# pypodman run --rm fedora date
2018-11-26 21:50:33 UTC | ERROR    | pypodman run: argument --rm: invalid choice: 'fedora' (choose from 'True', 'False')
2018-11-26 21:50:33 UTC | ERROR    | Try 'pypodman run --help' for more information.
```
Basically: even though podman itself doesn't require True/False, and `pypodman run --help` lists True/False as optional:
```
# pypodman run --help |grep -- --rm
                    [--read-only [{True,False}]] [--rm [{True,False}]]
  --rm [{True,False}]   Automatically remove the container when it exits.
```
...there doesn't seem to be a way to invoke pypodman with just `--rm` (or `-i`, or `--privileged`, or any other `BooleanAction` option).

python3-pypodman-0.11.1-1.gita4adfe5.fc29.noarch
